title: External Publications
description: Many RAND studies are published in peer-reviewed scholarly journals, as chapters in commercial books, or as documents published by other organizations.
text:
Process Evaluation of a Parish-Based Intervention to Reduce Mental Health-Related Stigma
In Latino Catholic communities, Project AMEN’s mental health activities were widely attended, well-received, and successful in reducing stigma, highlighting the power of church-based outreach.
Margaret D. Whitley, Maria Rosa Alvarado, Isabel Sierra, Brenda Scott, Vanessa N. Torres, Mario O. Martinez, Gabriela Castro, Yoselín Mayoral, Kathryn Pitkin Derose, Eunice C. Wong
Stay Tuned: Improving Sentiment Analysis and Stance Detection Using Large Language Models
We consider fine-tuning strategies to improve performance—including cross-target tuning using an assumption of congressmembers’ stance based on party affiliation—and strategies for fine-tuning LLMs, including few shot and chain-of-thought prompting.
Max Griswold, Michael W. Robbins, Michael S. Pollard
US Child Mental Health Care Need, Unmet Needs, and Difficulty Accessing Services
We used nationally representative data to quantify household-level receipt of and difficulty accessing mental health treatment for children.
Alyssa L. Halbisen, Joshua Breslau, Jonathan H. Cantor, Ryan K. McBain, Aaron Kofner, Mir M. Ali, Fang Zhang, Bradley D. Stein, Hao Yu
Trends in Medicaid Utilization and Financing for Hospitals With Psychiatric Beds
CMS HCRIS data from 2014 to 2023 were analyzed. Two Outcomes were examined: percentage of inpatient psychiatric discharges paid by Medicaid (both fee-for-service and managed care plans) and percentage of inpatient psychiatric days paid by Medicaid.
Jonathan H. Cantor, Aarya Suryavanshi, Aaron Kofner, Colleen M. McCullough, Megan S. Schuler, Christopher M. Whaley, Ryan K. McBain
CRC-SPIN Version 3.0: An Updated Policy Model for Colorectal Cancer Screening That Includes the Serrated Pathway
We describe version 3.0 of CRC-SPIN, which adds new information about the serrated pathway and CRC risk in adults under 50, then estimate the effectiveness of decennial colonoscopy from 45 to 75 years old.
Carolyn M. Rutter, Christopher E. Maerzluft, Laura Matrajt, Pedro Nascimento de Lima, Rachel B. Issaka, Tracey L. Marsh
Removing Tobacco Price Discounts from the Retail Point-of-Sale Exterior Reduces Young Adults’ Future Cigarette Smoking Susceptibility: An Experimental Investigation
The present study experimentally tested the impact of removing price discounts from a retail Point-of-Sale setting on young adults’ susceptibility to smoke cigarettes.
Michael S. Dunbar, Claude Messan Setodji, Steven C. Martino, Jody Chin Sing Wong, Grace Van Valkenburg, Desmond Jensen, William G. Shadel
Back-to-Basics Hospital-Wide Training Improves Inpatient Pediatric Care Experiences
We examined whether a hospital-wide Back-to-Basics training for clinical and nonclinical providers that focused on communication behaviors and safety protocols improved inpatient pediatric care experiences.
Denise D. Quigley, Maria Panayotou, Mary Ellen Slaughter
Europe’s Next War: The Rising Risk of NATO-Russia Conflict
As Russia’s war grinds on, NATO unity and European security hang in the balance. A cease-fire may bring renewed confrontation, making it vital to redefine ties with Moscow to avert another major conflict.
Samuel Charap, Hiski Haukkala
Racial and Ethnic Differences in Quitting When Marijuana Retail Markets Are Legal: Evidence from California
This research examines whether there is a corresponding difference in the response to the introduction of legal protection of marijuana retail markets.
Jenny Williams, Rosalie Liccardo Pacula, Rosanna Smart
Associations Between Historical Redlining and BMI: Potential Indirect Pathways Through Neighborhood Socioeconomic and Environment Factors and Their Associated Lifestyle Behaviors in CARDIA
We used data from the 1985-86 CARDIA study to test whether retrospective cohort associations between redlining and BMI were mediated by paths from census-derived social and economic neighborhood factors.
Andrea S. Richardson, Tamara Dubowitz, Feifei Ye, Kirsten M. M. Beyer, Yuhong Zhou, Kiarri Kershaw, Waverly O. Duck, Robin L. Beckman, James M. Shikany, Catarina I. Kiefe
